post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 244
Newbie Game 96


It is the year 2157. Fluorescent blue rain falls from the sky one day, dissolving everything around you. Cities fall, skyscrapers collapsing into piles of black sludge, and you run. You run and run and run, until you collapse on the ground and realize you don’t know where you are anymore. The rain keeps falling, drawing a veil around the world and leaving you wet and shivering. The only things you see are trees and bushes and grass, and muddy puddles that glow in the dark. You start to lose your sense of time and feel yourself getting number and number, until one day, you find yourself lying on your back, staring at an unfamiliar sky that thunders with blue lightning.

So when a giant ship appears in front of you one night and opens its doors, who are you to refuse?


- Important Rules -
This tab summarizes the most important rules.

For experienced players: this is the only tab you need to read.
For new players: if you have time, familiarizing yourself with the rest of the rules can be helpful.

Integrity
  • Do not edit or delete your posts, even for typos. Just post again.
  • Do not OOT (Out of Thread) and talk about this game outside your role PM, private chats, approved Discord channels, or this thread, even if you are dead. Access to the spectator chats will be granted upon request.
  • Do not gamethrow or intentionally sabotage your wincondition.
  • Do not attach files or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
  • Do not use OGI (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games.

Activity
  • Each "day" is 12 days while each "night" is 2 days.
  • Do not post during night.
  • This game uses Dawn start; Day 1 will not start until everyone shows that they have read the rules.
  • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable.
  • Activity rules are 48 hours to in-game prod and 72 hours to PM prod.

Voting
  • You must format your votes correctly. Capitalization doesn't matter, and capital i/lowercase L are not differentiated.
  • Majority hang is off for Day 1, but it is on starting Day 2.
  • Deadline hang is on from Day 1 but it is off for LYLO.
  • Votes are locked at LYLO.

Etiquette and Warnings
  • Use common sense.
  • Do not use formatting that makes text hard to read.
  • You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves.
  • First-timers can receive up to 3 warnings before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. All other players can receive up to 2.
  • All other rules are standard. If you break a rule that isn't covered here, you may still be warned.

Setup and Moderators
  • This game is based on the Matrix6 setup.
  • Mafia have access to daychat.
  • All role mechanics and Night Action Resolutions are standard.
  • This game is moderated by ad333 and mathjay.
  • This game operates optional discord spectator and mafia private chats using the Unpaid Intern Central server. PM the mods to request access.

Resources and Support
  • For any issues, you may contacts us through your Role PM or through Discord (단건우형 ㅠㅠ#5081/Nope but spooky#0907).
  • For a list of basic resources, please refer to the Resources tab below.
  • For an more extensive list of resources compiled by the UIMT, please go here.
  • If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer; you can ask us about this in your role PM.
  • Congratulations, you made it through the summary tab! Please discreetly state your favorite food below to secretly notify us that you've seen this message.

  • General
    Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas, among others, as well as the Mafia Universe forum rules, for the rule template.

    The following are standard mafia game rules.

    • Do not post during the night phase.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
    • Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent].
    • Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON.
    • If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate, and access to the spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered.
    • Do not impersonate the mods.
    • Do not lie to the mods.
    • Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.

  • Votes and Hangs
    This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.

    • Votes must be in this format: Vote: [player username] to be counted.
    • Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames.
    • For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical.
    • For example, both Vote: mathjay and Vote: mAtHjAy are valid votes, but vote: mathjay, Vote: jay, Vote: mathjay., Vote:mathjay, Vote: mathjay, and Vote: mathjay are invalid.
    • Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is bolded, even with separate bold tags, are invalid.
    • Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
    • Vote to hang no one by posting Vote: No Hang.
    • Unvote by posting Unvote. You do not have to unvote before voting again.
    • Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Please consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
    • Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
    • This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
    • If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If no votes have been cast by the deadline, no one will be hanged.
    • Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
    • Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
    • Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team.
    • The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
    • Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.
  • Activity
    This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.

    • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
    • Each day phase will be 288 hours (12 days), and each night phase will be 48 hours (2 days).
    • The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
    • If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform a NA that night.
    • Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
    • Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates in the case of a missed deadline.
    • Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in bold in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded.
    • V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for more options.
    • Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting Request Replacement in-thread or in their role PM.
    • Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in.
    • Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA.
    • Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
    • If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
    • If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
    • Activity will be reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart.
    • If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
    • If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
  • Integrity and Etiquette
    This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.

    • Do not gamethrow.
    • Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other interactions with moderators.
    • Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
    • Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably think as alignment indicative.

    The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, please click here.

    • Do not make personal attacks.
    • Do not spam.
    • Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
    • Follow AoPS Community Guidelines.
    • Be mature and use common sense.
    • For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.

    The above rules broadly cover most etiquette issues, but for specific details and examples of inconsiderate gameplay, please click here.

  • - Moderation Policies -
  • Moderator Information
    This tab provides technical information about this game.

    • ad333 and mathjay are the moderators of Mafia Game 244.
    • HannahW, Mathletesv, Scipio1, and Socoobo have contributed to flavor, setup, OCRSTM, feedback, and the management of the private chat discord system.
    • This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access to the server as appropriate.
    • Red text is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
    • Blue text is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators.
    • Green text is used for town flavor-related text.
    • Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes.
    • Current custom shades are: Town flavor green v.2 (#2be368), Activity Check blue (#005dff), mathjay's blue (#4a86e8), HannahW's blue (#0b5394), Mathletesv's blue (#4d94ff), Scipio1's blue (#3d70ff), Socoobo's orange (#ff8000), and ad333's green (#55d11f).
    • The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History.
    • Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules.
    • Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in OCRSTM Version History.
    • The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game.

    MG244OP is based on Overcomplicated RulesetTM (OCRSTM) 9.
    Current version: 9.4
    Version History
Setup
This game is based on the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Before the game starts, the setup will be randomly chosen from 6 possible role combinations:
  • Setup 1: 1 Town Jailkeeper, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup 2: 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
  • Setup 3: 1 Town Tracker, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup 4/A: 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
  • Setup 5/B: 1 Town Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
  • Setup 6/C: 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified:
  • This game is Dawn start. Once all the players confirm, Dawn will end, Day 1 will start, and the EOD deadline will be 14 days from then.
  • Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
  • A Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
  • A Mafia Roleblocker can kill and roleblock on the same night.
  • Cop results are sane.
  • Mafia have daychat.
Role PMs
Vanilla Townie Role PM wrote:


Hello, [player]!

You are a Wanter of a Good Apple Pie (Vanilla Townie). When the sky turned stormy and rain started to fall, you didn’t think much of it and merely took out the umbrella tucked inside your bag. Except, something wet fell on your head a moment later and you looked up to see your umbrella a metal skeleton, and even that wilted as you stared at it. You panicked, expecting the rain to burn and trying to find shelter, but after a while you realized it was just cold, rolling off your skin and soaking your hair just like regular rain. That’s when you noticed the building next to you sagging in your direction, people already shouting to get out of the way, and you ran as hard as you could. Eventually, you ended up sitting under a wizened old apple tree, hungry and looking at an apple on the ground. It must’ve only fallen recently, because it was perfect and a vivid red. You’d been careful not to swallow any of the rain so far, but you wondered if it would be okay to eat if you did your best to wipe it clean. Then the ship came.

Your power lies in your observation skills, your voice, and your vote.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

Click here to go to the game thread.
1-Shot Bulletproof Townie Role PM wrote:


Hello, [player]!

You are the Owner of a Cyborg Hamster (1-Shot Bulletproof Townie). You used to work at a big biomedical company. You forgot the name, but it was researching neurological implants and created a lot of prototypes. Somehow, you ended up one of them. It was a hamster with a metal skeleton and puppy-like intelligence, designated ECBO-215. You named it Wubbo, and it likes raspberries. You should ask the ship if it has some.

You will survive a single night kill attempt by the mafia because Wubbo will jump in front of you and protect you, but if you are shot again after that, you will be killed. You are not immune to the daily hang, your ability cannot be roleblocked, and you do not know if or when you are shot.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

Click here to go to the game thread.
Town Cop Role PM wrote:


Hello, [player]!

You are the Small Child (Town Cop). Your father had wandered off somewhere like he usually does and he wasn’t picking up, so your mother had gone to find him. She gave you a box of grape juice and her phone, which you knew was locked. Then she told you to stay put and wait for her, and ran towards the lake, disappearing over the hill. When the rain came, you watched in fascination as it leeched all the colors from your juice box, purple and silver droplets splattering on the ground, leaving behind a soggy lump of brown cardboard. Then you sat under a large oak tree and waited, dozing off several times. When the ship appeared in front of you, you boarded it before you realized you didn’t have your mother’s phone anymore.

No one really thinks too much about a child wandering where they shouldn’t. Once each night, you may PM the mods the name of a player, and you will snoop through their rooms to see if they have anything suspicious. You will be informed of their alignment, or whether your action was blocked.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

Click here to go to the game thread.
Town Doctor Role PM wrote:


Hello, [player]!

You are the Sleep-Deprived EMT (Town Doctor). The last thing you remember is boarding the first train home after your shift ended and falling asleep. You woke up because something was hitting your face and found yourself in the rain, trapped up to your chest in silvery mud. It took hours for you to work yourself out because it was so dense and heavy, and once you were free, you collapsed under a rocky overhang in exhaustion. Later, when you went back out to see where you were, you didn’t recognize anything. You only found random scraps of fabric and a worn leather journal that was, strangely enough, blank. Confused, you went back. When you finally saw a light in the distance, you ran towards it, yelling and waving your hands. It was the ship.

Once each night, you may PM the mods the name of a player, and you will fall asleep outside their door. If they’re attacked in the middle of the night, you’ll be there to stop the bleeding however you can until the ship wakes up again and hopefully finds a way to heal them. You can save yourself, but you cannot do this two nights in a row.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

Click here to go to the game thread.
Town Jailkeeper Role PM wrote:


Hello, [player]!

You are the Aging Gardener with a Sense of Humor (Town Jailkeeper). You were watering plants on a long-time client’s estate when it started to rain, when it really shouldn’t have rained, and the watering hose dissolved in your hands. And then you tried to find your way back to the manor, only to get lost. It was only hours later that you realized that it must’ve been washed away by the rain as well. When the rainship found you, you were sitting in front of some wild, overgrown shrubs in bloom, and you took some with you. Common milkweed makes your stomach want to die. Wild senna is much harder to find, but it’s a very, very good laxative.

Once each night, you may PM the mods the name of a player, and you will spike their food with a special End of the World edition of your Plant Punch Potion. The food is unfamiliar enough that they won’t question the taste, but it’ll give them enough problems to lock them up in a bathroom for most of the night. They will be unable to perform any night actions, but they will also be protected from any night kills that target them that night. You cannot target yourself with this ability because you’re too old to set off a bomb in your stomach.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

Click here to go to the game thread.
Town Tracker Role PM wrote:


Hello, [player]!

You are the Person with a Spider in Their Room (Town Tracker). You found a spider in the room the ship told you to use. Or maybe it had hitched a ride somewhere, hidden in the folds of your clothes and followed you in. Either way, you find yourself wondering how long it’ll survive as you watch it spin a web in the corner of the room, because you haven’t seen a single fly or mosquito during your entire stay on the ship. Maybe it’ll be able to eat the ship’s food if you break off a tiny piece and stick it on its web?

Once each night, you may PM the mods the name of a player. You will carefully string a spiderweb across their doorway. That way, you will know if the player visited anyone during the night when you wake up and check if the web is gone. You will also know who they targeted with their night action, if any, once you find the web again. You will be informed if your action was blocked.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

Click here to go to the game thread.
Mafia Goon Role PM wrote:


Hello, [player]!

You are an Old Neighbor (Mafia Goon). When the rain came, you were fine. You built your house with a nail, a hammer, and your own two hands, and you did a darn fine job too. The corporations and the government have been after your land for years, but you refused to give in like a city coward. You only boarded the ship because you ran out of water, and you weren’t drinking the mad science experiment falling from the sky. In fact, you wouldn’t be surprised if this was all an elaborate scheme to steal what’s rightfully yours. Don’t think for a moment that you haven’t noticed the others muttering together when they think you aren’t looking. Once they turn on you and show their true colors, you’ll be ready with your shotgun.

Your partner is [player] ([role]), and you may discuss with them in this private forum while you are alive. The two of you will be able to choose a player to kill each night, as well as decide who makes the kill.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

Click here to go to the game thread.
Mafia Roleblocker Role PM wrote:


Hello, [player]!

You are the Lost Agent (Mafia Roleblocker). You’re lost. Your objective has disappeared and likely dissolved. None of your communications chips are working, and the hidden chargers that powered them are all gone with the rain. Once your implants run out of battery, you’ll be as good as dead, and you only have a couple days left. You can’t even think too loudly because you’re not sure the chip masking your brainwaves still works, and you don’t trust this ship, not even one bit. Who built it? What even is its true purpose? Or were you just captured during your mission, and all of this is just an interrogation simulation?

Once each night, you may PM the mods the name of a player. You will then release sleeping gas in their room using the sublimation kernels hidden in your teeth. This will knock them out, and any actions they attempt that night will be blocked. Your action takes precedence over the Town Jailkeeper, if applicable.

Your partner is [player] (Mafia Goon), and you may discuss with them in this private forum while you are alive. The two of you will be able to choose a player to kill each night, as well as decide who makes the kill. You can simultaneously perform both the factional kill and your roleblock.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

Click here to go to the game thread.

[list][*]For any issues, you may contacts us through your Role PM or through Discord (단건우형 ㅠㅠ#5081/Nope but spooky#0907). 
[*]For a list of basic resources, please refer to the Resources tab below. 
[*]For an more extensive list of resources compiled by the UIMT, please go [url=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/c1980342h2517106_important_resources]here[/url].
[*]If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer; you can ask us about this in your role PM. 
[*]Congratulations, you made it through the summary tab! Please discreetly state your favorite food below to secretly notify us that you've seen this message. [/list][/hide]

[list][*][hide=General]
[i]Credits to [url=http://bit.ly/fbith]Binomial-theorem[/url], [url=http://bit.ly/rmchao]chaotic_iak[/url], [url=http://bit.ly/rmww123]willwang123[/url], and [url=http://bit.ly/ffdas]fdas[/url], among others, as well as the [url=http://bit.ly/MUnFR]Mafia Universe forum rules[/url], for the rule template.[/i]

[b]The following are standard mafia game rules.[/b]


[list][*]Do not post during the night phase.
[*]Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
[*]Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
[*]Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [verbatim][color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent][/verbatim]. 
[*]Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON. 
[*]If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate, and access to the spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered. 
[*]Do not impersonate the mods. 
[*]Do not lie to the mods. 
[*]Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement. 
[*]You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.[/list][/hide]

[*][hide=Votes and Hangs]
[b]This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.[/b]


[list][*]Votes must be in this format: [b]Vote: [player username][/b] to be counted.
[*]Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames. 
[*]For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical. 
[*]For example, both [b]Vote: mathjay[/b] and [b]Vote: mAtHjAy[/b] are valid votes, but [b]vote: mathjay[/b], [b]Vote: jay[/b], [b]Vote: mathjay.[/b], [b]Vote:mathjay[/b], Vote: [b]mathjay[/b], and [b][i]Vote: mathjay[/i][/b] are invalid. 
[*]Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is bolded, even with separate bold tags, are invalid. 
[*]Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
[*]Vote to hang no one by posting [b]Vote: No Hang[/b].
[*]Unvote by posting [b]Unvote[/b]. You do not have to unvote before voting again. 
[*]Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Please consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
[*]Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
[*]This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged. 
[*]If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If no votes have been cast by the deadline, no one will be hanged. 
[*]Starting Day 2, if a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
[*]Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
[*]Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team. 
[*]The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
[*]Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.[/list][/hide]
[*][hide=Activity]
[b]This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.[/b]


[list][*]All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
[*]Each day phase will be 288 hours (12 days), and each night phase will be 48 hours (2 days). 
[*]The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
[*]If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform a NA that night.
[*]Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
[*]Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates in the case of a missed deadline. 
[*]Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded. 
[*]V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for more options. 
[*]Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting [b]Request Replacement[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. 
[*]Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in. 
[*]Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
[*]Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA. 
[*]Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement. 
[*]If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
[*]If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
[*]Activity will be reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart. 
[*]If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
[*]If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.[list][/hide]
[*][hide=Integrity and Etiquette]
[b]This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.[/b]


[list][*]Do not gamethrow. 
[*]Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other interactions with moderators. 
[*]Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
[*]Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably think as alignment indicative. [/list]

The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, [url=https://aops.com/community/p23135586]please click here.[/url]


[list][*]Do not make personal attacks.
[*]Do not spam. 
[*]Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning. 
[*]Follow AoPS Community Guidelines. 
[*]Be mature and use common sense. 
[*]For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.[/list]

The above rules broadly cover most etiquette issues, but for specific details and examples of inconsiderate gameplay, [url=https://aops.com/community/p23135588]please click here.[/url][/hide]

[*][b][url=https://aops.com/community/p23135593]- Moderation Policies -[/url][/b]

[*][hide=Moderator Information]
[b]This tab provides technical information about this game.[/b]


[list][*]ad333 and mathjay are the moderators of Mafia Game 244. 
[*]HannahW, Mathletesv, Scipio1, and Socoobo have contributed to flavor, setup, OCRSTM, feedback, and the management of the private chat discord system. 
[*]This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access to the server as appropriate. 
[*][color=#FF0000]Red text[/color] is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
[*][color=#0000FF]Blue text[/color] is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators. 
[*][color=#00FF00]Green text[/color] is used for town flavor-related text.
[*]Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes. 
[*]Current custom shades are: Town flavor green v.2 ([b][color=#2be368]#2be368[/color][/b]), Activity Check blue ([b][color=#005dff]#005dff[/color][/b]), mathjay's blue ([b][color=#4a86e8]#4a86e8[/color][/b]), HannahW's blue ([b][color=#0b5394]#0b5394[/color][/b]), Mathletesv's blue ([b][color=#4d94ff]#4d94ff[/color][/b]), Scipio1's blue ([b][color=#3d70ff]#3d70ff[/color][/b]), Socoobo's orange ([b][color=#ff8000]#ff8000[/color][/b]), and ad333's green ([b][color=#55d11f]#55d11f[/color][/b]).
[*]The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
[*]If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
[*]The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules. 
[*]Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game. [/list]

[b]MG244OP is based on Overcomplicated Ruleset[sup]TM[/sup] (OCRSTM) 9.[/b]
[i]Current version: 9.4[/i]
[url=https://aops.com/community/p21335350]Version History[/url]
[/hide][/list]
[hide=Setup]This game is based on the [url=http://bit.ly/m6msw]Matrix6[/url] setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Before the game starts, the setup will be randomly chosen from 6 possible role combinations:
[list][*][b]Setup 1:[/b] 1 Town Jailkeeper, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*][b]Setup 2:[/b] 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*][b]Setup 3:[/b] 1 Town Tracker, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*][b]Setup 4/A:[/b] 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-shot Bulletproof Townie, 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*][b]Setup 5/B:[/b] 1 Town Cop, 6 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons
[*][b]Setup 6/C:[/b] 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 5 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons[/list]
There are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified: 
[list][*]This game is Dawn start. Once all the players confirm, Dawn will end, Day 1 will start, and the EOD deadline will be 14 days from then. 
[*]Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
[*]A Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
[*]A Mafia Roleblocker can kill and roleblock on the same night.
[*]Cop results are sane.
[*]Mafia have daychat.[/list]
[hide=Role PMs]
[quote=Vanilla Townie Role PM][img]https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/763965102008696883/907659062785749092/IMG_1961.jpg[/img]

Hello, [player]!

You are a [b][color=#2be368]Wanter of a Good Apple Pie (Vanilla Townie)[/color][/b]. When the sky turned stormy and rain started to fall, you didn’t think much of it and merely took out the umbrella tucked inside your bag. Except, something wet fell on your head a moment later and you looked up to see your umbrella a metal skeleton, and even that wilted as you stared at it. You panicked, expecting the rain to burn and trying to find shelter, but after a while you realized it was just cold, rolling off your skin and soaking your hair just like regular rain. That’s when you noticed the building next to you sagging in your direction, people already shouting to get out of the way, and you ran as hard as you could. Eventually, you ended up sitting under a wizened old apple tree, hungry and looking at an apple on the ground. It must’ve only fallen recently, because it was perfect and a vivid red. You’d been careful not to swallow any of the rain so far, but you wondered if it would be okay to eat if you did your best to wipe it clean. Then the ship came. 

Your power lies in your observation skills, your voice, and your vote. 

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

[url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]Click here to go to the game thread.[/url]
[/quote]
[quote=1-Shot Bulletproof Townie Role PM]
[img]https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/763965102008696883/907659062785749092/IMG_1961.jpg[/img]

Hello, [player]!

You are the [b][color=#2be368]Owner of a Cyborg Hamster (1-Shot Bulletproof Townie)[/color][/b]. You used to work at a big biomedical company. You forgot the name, but it was researching neurological implants and created a lot of prototypes. Somehow, you ended up one of them. It was a hamster with a metal skeleton and puppy-like intelligence, designated ECBO-215. You named it Wubbo, and it likes raspberries. You should ask the ship if it has some. 

You will survive a single night kill attempt by the mafia because Wubbo will jump in front of you and protect you, but if you are shot again after that, you will be killed. You are not immune to the daily hang, your ability cannot be roleblocked, and you do not know if or when you are shot. 

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

[url=http://bit.ly/2hmph2]Click here to go to the game thread.[/url]

[quote=Town Cop Role PM]
[img]https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/763965102008696883/907659062785749092/IMG_1961.jpg[/img]

Hello, [player]!

You are the [b][color=#2be368]Small Child (Town Cop)[/color][/b]. Your father had wandered off somewhere like he usually does and he wasn’t picking up, so your mother had gone to find him. She gave you a box of grape juice and her phone, which you knew was locked. Then she told you to stay put and wait for her, and ran towards the lake, disappearing over the hill. When the rain came, you watched in fascination as it leeched all the colors from your juice box, purple and silver droplets splattering on the ground, leaving behind a soggy lump of brown cardboard. Then you sat under a large oak tree and waited, dozing off several times. When the ship appeared in front of you, you boarded it before you realized you didn’t have your mother’s phone anymore. 

No one really thinks too much about a child wandering where they shouldn’t. Once each night, you may PM the mods the name of a player, and you will snoop through their rooms to see if they have anything suspicious. You will be informed of their alignment, or whether your action was blocked. 

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

[quote=Town Doctor Role PM]
[img]https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/763965102008696883/907659062785749092/IMG_1961.jpg[/img]

Hello, [player]!

You are the [b][color=#2be368]Sleep-Deprived EMT (Town Doctor)[/color][/b]. The last thing you remember is boarding the first train home after your shift ended and falling asleep. You woke up because something was hitting your face and found yourself in the rain, trapped up to your chest in silvery mud. It took hours for you to work yourself out because it was so dense and heavy, and once you were free, you collapsed under a rocky overhang in exhaustion. Later, when you went back out to see where you were, you didn’t recognize anything. You only found random scraps of fabric and a worn leather journal that was, strangely enough, blank. Confused, you went back. When you finally saw a light in the distance, you ran towards it, yelling and waving your hands. It was the ship. 

Once each night, you may PM the mods the name of a player, and you will fall asleep outside their door. If they’re attacked in the middle of the night, you’ll be there to stop the bleeding however you can until the ship wakes up again and hopefully finds a way to heal them. You can save yourself, but you cannot do this two nights in a row. 

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

[quote=Town Jailkeeper Role PM]
[img]https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/763965102008696883/907659062785749092/IMG_1961.jpg[/img]

Hello, [player]!

You are the [b][color=#2be368]Aging Gardener with a Sense of Humor (Town Jailkeeper)[/color][/b]. You were watering plants on a long-time client’s estate when it started to rain, when it really shouldn’t have rained, and the watering hose dissolved in your hands. And then you tried to find your way back to the manor, only to get lost. It was only hours later that you realized that it must’ve been washed away by the rain as well. When the rainship found you, you were sitting in front of some wild, overgrown shrubs in bloom, and you took some with you. Common milkweed makes your stomach want to die. Wild senna is much harder to find, but it’s a very, very good laxative. 

Once each night, you may PM the mods the name of a player, and you will spike their food with a special End of the World edition of your Plant Punch Potion. The food is unfamiliar enough that they won’t question the taste, but it’ll give them enough problems to lock them up in a bathroom for most of the night. They will be unable to perform any night actions, but they will also be protected from any night kills that target them that night. You cannot target yourself with this ability because you’re too old to set off a bomb in your stomach. 

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

[quote=Town Tracker Role PM]
[img]https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/763965102008696883/907659062785749092/IMG_1961.jpg[/img]

Hello, [player]!

You are the [b][color=#2be368]Person with a Spider in Their Room (Town Tracker)[/color][/b]. You found a spider in the room the ship told you to use. Or maybe it had hitched a ride somewhere, hidden in the folds of your clothes and followed you in. Either way, you find yourself wondering how long it’ll survive as you watch it spin a web in the corner of the room, because you haven’t seen a single fly or mosquito during your entire stay on the ship. Maybe it’ll be able to eat the ship’s food if you break off a tiny piece and stick it on its web? 

Once each night, you may PM the mods the name of a player. You will carefully string a spiderweb across their doorway. That way, you will know if the player visited anyone during the night when you wake up and check if the web is gone. You will also know who they targeted with their night action, if any, once you find the web again. You will be informed if your action was blocked. 

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

[quote=Mafia Goon Role PM]
[img]https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/763965102008696883/907659062785749092/IMG_1961.jpg[/img]

Hello, [player]!

You are an [b][color=red]Old Neighbor (Mafia Goon)[/color][/b]. When the rain came, you were fine. You built your house with a nail, a hammer, and your own two hands, and you did a darn fine job too. The corporations and the government have been after your land for years, but you refused to give in like a city coward. You only boarded the ship because you ran out of water, and you weren’t drinking the mad science experiment falling from the sky. In fact, you wouldn’t be surprised if this was all an elaborate scheme to steal what’s rightfully yours. Don’t think for a moment that you haven’t noticed the others muttering together when they think you aren’t looking. Once they turn on you and show their true colors, you’ll be ready with your shotgun. 

Your partner is [b][color=red][player] ([role])[/color][/b], and you may discuss with them in [url=http://bit.ly/MafQT]this private forum[/url] while you are alive. The two of you will be able to choose a player to kill each night, as well as decide who makes the kill. 

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

Hello, [player]!

You are the [b][color=red]Lost Agent (Mafia Roleblocker)[/color][/b]. You’re lost. Your objective has disappeared and likely dissolved. Once your implants run out of battery, you’ll be as good as dead, and you only have a couple days left. You can’t even think too loudly because you’re not sure the chip masking your brainwaves still works, and you don’t trust this ship, not even one bit. Who built it? What even is its true purpose? Your partner is [b][color=red][player] (Mafia Goon)[/color][/b], and you may discuss with them in [url=http://bit.ly/MafQT]this private forum[/url] while you are alive. The two of you will be able to choose a player to kill each night, as well as decide who makes the kill. You can simultaneously perform both the factional kill and your roleblock. 

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

post_rendered:
When the ship calls everyone to the main cabin a little before dinner, you don’t think much of it. But then you bump into Jane out in the hallway. She’s staring at the other end, where a narrow spiral staircase leads to the bathrooms. “Hey, what’s up?” you say, walking up to her.

“Nothing,” she says. She pauses. “Did you hear those gunshots last night?”

“Um, I don’t think so?” you say, surprised. “But I am a pretty heavy sleeper. Why, did someone get shot?”

“I don’t know,” she replies. “You haven’t seen Cory today, have you?”

You shake your head.

Jane clicks her tongue. “I don’t like this. Let me just check something. Can you come with..?”

“Okay,” you agree, and you follow her down the staircase.

* * * * *

“Good evening, everyone, and thank you for coming. Please welcome our newest survivor aboard. What would you like us to call you?”

“Isaiah,” says the young man standing awkwardly in the middle of the room. He is pale, shivering slightly, and is drying himself off with a towel. Pal - the cleaning robot that ends up doing most of the work taking care of them - is already gliding over with a steaming cup. “You can call me Isaiah.”

“Hello, Isaiah,” says the voice. “I am Cal, and I am the one who found you last night. I’m sure you have a lot of questions, so I’ll start by answering the big ones. But please, drink the broth. It’ll help you warm up. You can also sit anywhere you like.

“As you can guess, the rain outside is not natural. It has been contaminated by a chemical compound called A2ANX, which will dissolve a spectrum of inorganic materials that it comes into contact with. It also appears to have unusual effects on plants and animals after prolonged exposure. A2ANX’s existence was a closely guarded state secret, and its origins are classified; it was, however, sought after by a number of countries. I do not actually know how it ended up spreading over such a wide area, but a reasonable guess is that it was the result of a conflict between the United Americas and the Sino-Pacific Federation.

“I am a rainship. My hull is made out of a unique material resistant to A2ANX, and I have enough power and resources to care for any passengers for a few months. I do not know for what purpose I was built, but since my activation I have been searching for survivors and providing shelter until we reach a safe harbor. There has been nothing so far, nor have I been able to establish communications with anyone. I am also technically unfinished: you are free to go wherever you wish, but there are a few rooms that are locked for your own safety. I will also shut down for a few hours every night for maintenance, during which I ask you to stay in your rooms.

“I think that’s enough for now, and dinner’s almost ready. Do you have any questions?”

“Just one,” says Isaiah. “Who are you? You said, ‘I am a rainship,’ but I wasn’t sure. Are you the captain, or...”

“Oh, no,” Cal replies, laughing gently. “I apologize, I should have said this when I introduced myself. I am the ship’s consciousness. An artificial intelligence, if you will. It was a pleasure to make your acquaintance.”

Hello newbies! I'm here to help you guys out with this game.
First of all, READ THE IMPORTANT RULES TAB. As long as you can follow these rules everything should be fine. If you have any trouble determining what a rule means (e.g. OOT can be hard to understand as a newbie) then ask fdas, me, or awesomeming.

Also, make sure to confirm. If you thoroughly read all of the rules, then you should have seen a message that tells you how to confirm. Although it may not exactly say "do <something> to confirm", if you keep a sharp eye out you should see something out of place. That will be the confirm rule's instructions!

MOST IMPORTANT RULES TO REMEMBER

These rules are usually the ones that newbies mess up the most, so make sure to try and follow these rules above all else.
  • DO NOT SPOIL THE CONFIRM RULE.
  • DO NOT SPEAK OF THIS GAME OUTSIDE OF THE THREAD. (Tip: Pretend this game doesn't exist when posting anywhere else in AoPS)
  • DO NOT EDIT OR DELETE YOUR POSTS. (Due to game integrity purposes, editing and deleting posts are not allowed)
  • DO NOT MAKE PERSONAL ATTACKS. (This is saying stuff like "how did you make it pass grade school with these social skills" and the like)
  • DO NOT POST IF YOU ARE DEAD OR POST DURING THE NIGHT.
  • DON'T TAKE WORDS TO HEART. (Sometimes people may say slightly rude things, but in the end they don't really mean it. Just remind yourself that this is a game and if you get heated take a break)
  • MAKE SURE TO FORMAT YOUR VOTES CORRECTLY. (Read the rules for voting if you are unsure how to vote)

And that's it! Let the games begin.

Guides:

Voting/Hanging FAQ - PLEASE READ
Why do we not No Hang on Day 1?

Because we lose the ability to eliminate an extra person.
Here's the math:
(I'm going under the assumption that we mishang, or wrongly hang, a townie every time we hang per day.)

7-2 (7 townspeople, 2 mafia)
We no hang D1.
Scum (other term for mafia) kills a townsperson.
6-2
We hang a townsperson.
Scum kills a townie (other term for townsperson/people).
4-2
We hang another townie.
Scum kills a townie.
2-2 (Town loses! Reminder that if the number of scum equals or is greater than the number of townies left, scum wins)
In this theoretical scenario, we only use 2 hangs before we lose.
However, look at when we don't no hang D1.

7-2 (7 townspeople, 2 mafia)
We hang D1.
Scum kills N1.
5-2
We hang D2.
Scum kills N2.
3-2
We hang a townie D3.
2-2
As you can see, we use up 3 hangs this time instead of two, so it is just mechanically better to hang D1.

What is LyLo and MyLo?

LyLo is hang or lose and MyLo is mishang or lose. Simply put, LyLo is when town must hang scum in order to survive, and MyLo is when it is mechanically better (although sometimes not the best choice) for town to no hang to bring town to LyLo. For example, 3-2 is LyLo and 4-2 is MyLo. In MyLo, when town no hangs scum is forced to kill a person which can narrow down the options for who is scum.

What are locked votes and why do they matter?

Locked votes mean that once you cast a vote, you cannot change it. This will be applied in LyLo of this game, so make sure to be cautious once you enter a LyLo situation, because once you make your vote it is permanent.

What's the difference between majority and deadline hang?

Majority hang is when if town reaches a majority vote on one player, that player will be hanged before deadline ends.
Deadline hang is when the leading wagon at the end of the day is hanged even if the wagon reached majority before deadline.

So, we cannot quickly hang someone on D1 and LyLo but we can on D2 and other days (that are not LyLo).


aroodman's op guide - please read

How to Hunt scum - Do not need to read but it will help even if you just skim it
No this is not copypasta'ed from MU, I made it myself.

Scumhunting is usually portrayed as complicated and rigorous, especially to new newbies of the forum. However, it actually relies mostly on intuition and processing actions people make in the game. There are a few basic fundamentals that anyone who plays mafia should learn and more importantly, understand. Understanding why these tells work and how to put them to use allows you to combine tells or slightly alter them depending on the context of which you are applying these scum tells in. With this basic toolkit, you can master Mafia by applying them in games and practicing them frequently.

Please read the hide tags which explain these scumtells, as you may misuse these tells if you take them for granted.

I. Experience
The most powerful tool of mafia is experience, and applying patterns you see in different games allows you to easily recognize the difference between scum and town.


II. Motivation tells
Scum want to kill townspeople and find ways to hang town instead of scum, whether as townies want to kill scum. If you put yourself in another person's shoes and consider why they made a specific post, you may be able to gain insight on if this person is town or scum (This tell is referenced as a Motivation tell).


III. Night Kill Analysis
Tread carefully with NKA! There are times when NKA is a dangerous ground to study, and there can be lots of WIFOM involved.

Night kills are a way for scum to quickly eliminate town, but townies (townspeople, vanilla townies, etc.) can also glean information from NK's (night kills). There are infinitely many reasons for why scum could NK a specific person, however consider probability over possibility, as scum are far less likely to actually kill a person for a random reason like "he/she thought that I was mafia from this one post at the very beginning of the game". Consider each of your suspects making the kill, and see if it makes some sense for them to kill this person. Killing that person for the sake of killing them is not good logic and you must think probability over possibility. (This tell is referenced as Night Kill Analysis, or NKA)


IV. PoE
Sometimes finding town is more effective then finding scum. If you successfully identify every townie, then the remaining people who you don't think are a townie must be scum. "How do you find townies?" is a common question, and the straightforward answer is that you must apply all of the other scumhunting tells but to try and identify if a person is town instead of trying to identify if a person is scum. (This process is called PoE)


V. Meta
Meta is not as important or as powerful as any of the other tells in this list. Some people have used it as a crutch to make their reads, but do not follow their example. Meta is only used to support a read lightly with previous games, as patterns can change and habit can disappear if the player you are analyzing makes these changes in the ongoing game.

Make sure that you do not OOT with this tell by reviewing games that are still ongoing.

This tell is not that important to learn, especially if you are just a beginner in Mafia, but it is a good tool to understand, as people occasionally use it and you may want to debate over that read given your opinion about it.

Meta is simply the process of reviewing previous games that a person played in, and comparing their behavior in the previous game with the current game you are playing. It is best if you compare a game where this person was town and a game where this person was scum, so that you can get the full spectrum of their habits as both alignments.

For example, if a person was scum in another game and tunneled (basically accused someone hard and stuck with their scumread for a long time) and they are tunneling in the ongoing game, you can use this meta analysis to support any scumreads you have on them.

However, your reason for comparison must be significant, not just one post or a single analysis where he/she made specific reads that also happen to be the same in the ongoing game.


Practically 90% of all reads form every read in Mafia, with some different combinations here and there. There are other "tells", however I disagree that these are actually true scumtells when used.

i. Gut reads
Literally making a opinion on someone based on nothing, or very little evidence. These types of reads are almost always just random thoughts based on nothing, so pay them little heed when they are presented.


ii. Tone reads
Practically the same as gut reads in every way, shape, and form, however they are based on "tone of voice." Tone usually isn't AI unless its AtE (I'll cover this in another hide tag), so these kinds of reads are usually worthless too.


iii. >randtown/>randscum
"randtown" and "randscum" represent the chance that someone has randed town or randed scum. >randtown/>randscum represent that someone thinks that a player is has a greater chance of being town or scum, but this chance is usually a small improvement, so reads that have >randtown/>randscum attached to them are not supposed to be taken very seriously.


iv. GtH
Gun-to-Head, or GtH, is making snap decisions on who is town and who is scum. Most of the time, the majority of these reads have very little meaning to them and GtH isn't very effective in regular games. A more meaningful tell is that scum usually are the ones who initiate a GtH, because people think they are a easy point of discussion to base off of.


v. Behavioral patterns that did not originate from a mafia game
This kind of read is rarer, since most Mafia players rarely play with anyone else in the forum outside of Mafia, but I noticed it in a couple of newbie games. If you aren't studying meta, then behavioral patterns are NAI when considering external sources. For example, if your friend plays games in Island of the Lost or some other AoPS forum, their tone there is not applicable as a read. This is because Mafia is a extremely unique game which requires people to change how they talk and speak to others in the game, which means that your friend will likely not use a tone that they would use in IOTL.


AtE

AtE, or Appeal to Emotion, is being extremely emotional about your position in the game and sometimes players self-vote and give up trying to save themselves from being hanged. Reading AtE is similar to NKA, it can be a massive WIFOM minefield, so tread carefully when analyzing AtE!

What to look for in AtE
There are significant differences between town AtE and scum AtE. It's hard to describe all of it in words, as the tone is usually the key to reading AtE, but I'll give a few pointers on what to look for:

- Meta does play a part in AtE, as people who AtE often have easy patterns you can see when comparing different games.

- Newbies who are town that AtE usually involve self-voting and then going non-existent. I personally think it is rare for newbies who are town to really go all out to defend themselves. These newbies who are extremely concerned about self-preservation and image are almost always scum.

- Town AtE in general varies from player to player. The most important actions to look for are people who getting genuinely frustrated and then start gloating over the fact that they got hanged. How do you determine when someone is genuinely frustrated? It's hard to describe, and it really depends on their tone and how they refute the reasons for why people are hanging them.

- Experienced players rarely AtE, so if one does, keep a wary eye on them. AtE is also the last resort for scum as well, and most experienced players who AtE are trying to barely squeeze their way out from the edge of the cliff.

- Scum who AtE get really emotional and sometimes go overboard with the amount of emotion they try to put into their posts. For example, if someone dramatically overreacts and continually self-votes, they are likely scum. Again, meta exceptions apply, but just by analyzing AtE, people who do these kinds of things are usually scum.


Commonly used Mafia Terms - Use this to pinpoint terms that you don't understand
Created by Mafia Universe
Edited by EulerNTM
Number terms
#-Shot - When a role can only be used a certain amount of times during a game, with # representing that number.

3P - Third Party - Players that are not aligned with the town or mafia.

A
AI - Alignment Indicative - an action or post by a player that IS indicative of whether they are aligned with the town or mafia

Angleshooting - Angleshooting or to "angleshoot" is violating the spirit of the rules without actually breaking the rules. Angleshooting is not technically cheating, but is considered equally unacceptable. Explained in depth on the forum rules post.

Anti-spew - Usually used to refer to when a mafia-aligned player is very close to being killed and goes into “anti-spew”, where they are careful about their wording so that town are not able to determine who is/isn’t their partner(s) after they flip mafia.

AtE - Appeal to Emotion - When a player makes a post in order to evoke sympathy so that they can avoid being hanged.

B
Bandwagon - When player(s) hop onto a vote wagon.

BM - Short for "Bastard Mod", who can lie to players about game mechanics (including in their role PM), uses a relatively screwed setup where players might change roles, alliances, and sometimes with messed up, new roles. Generally not good for a newbie game.

BP - Bulletproof - When a player is immune to attack(s) during the night phase or during ITAs, either because they have a bulletproof modifier or a bulletproof vest.

BPV - Bulletproof Vest - A vest which is bulletproof, usually only takes one shot.

Breadcrumbing - When a player leaves clues as to what their role is/what their night actions are, in case they die without the information being revealed.

Bussing - when a mafia-aligned player votes or pushes a fellow mafia-aligned player in order to appear as if they are town-aligned whenever the mafia-aligned player they are voting has their role revealed.

C
CC - Counterclaim - when a player claims the same role as another player.

Cemetery - a place where dead players can talk about the game freely. Also known as the graveyard, DVC, or Dead Villager Chat.

CFD - Chinese Fire Drill - When a group of players suddenly switch their vote somewhere else at last minute, usually another player who wasn’t seen as an option for the hang at that point in the phase.

Claim Fishing - When a player tries to get another player to reveal their role, usually done in a subtle fashion.

Crossvoting - when two people are voting on each other. Usually in reference to MYLO or LYLO states where if Person A is voted by Person B, then either the game is over if they're both town (as mafia-aligned players can vote over Player B with majority) or there's confirmed a mafia-aligned player between the two of them.

Counterwagon - the person with the second highest amount of votes other than the person who was hanged, or in larger or more intricate games a person with enough votes on them to be considered in contention for the hang.

D
D1/D2… etc. - Day phase

DADV - Dead Air, Dead Villager - When communication in the thread has gone down, so players infer that it’s because the hang is a villager.

Derpclear - When someone seemingly shows a lack of knowledge about an open/semi-open setup, or any other information that a mafia-aligned player would be expected to know, and is then cleared for this reason by another player.

Distancing - When two mafia-aligned players try to make it seem like they are not aligned with each other.

DVC - Dead Villager Chat, otherwise known as the graveyard or cemetery. Despite the name, dead wolves are generally allowed to participate.

E
EOD - End of Day

F
F3/F5/F# - A point in the game at which # of players are alive. Generally, but not always, either used to describe a LYLO or MYLO situation or one where there is otherwise significantly less players alive than there were at game start.

Fakeclaiming - When a player claims a role that they do not have.

Flavor - The game’s theme, sometimes what roles are based off of, and creates a story usually based on what happens in the game.

Fluffposting/Fluffing - When a player posts nothing of content.

FPS - Fancy Play Syndrome - When a player uses an elaborate strategy over a more simple one.

Frozen - When a player has stopped posting, often after being called out as mafia in the thread.
Alternative: Thread is frozen and no one is posting.

G
Gambit - Any play which is made in order to evoke a certain reaction, which can be made from both alignments.

Gamethrowing - When a player actively makes an action to intentionally throw the game for their alignment; examples of this can include mafia hardclaiming mafia without a strategic reason and/or revealing who the mafia team is in the thread.

GOAT - Greatest of All Time

H
Hammer - A vote that’s placed on a player which causes the wagon to reach majority in order to hang the player that the wagon is on, ending the day phase after the vote is placed.

Hard Claim - to claim a role with there being no uncertain terms - see Soft Claim, is relevant.

Hedging - When a player does not commit to a stance and stays on the fence regarding a topic, usually the alignment of a player.

Hydra - An account shared by two or more players that share a role and have their own private chat.

I
ISO - Isolating a specific player’s posts in order to look at all of them in one place.

ITA - In Thread Attack - A mechanic used in most mashes where every player has a X% hit rate shot they may use in the thread during designated times to attempt to kill someone.

ITT - In The Thread

J
JOAT - Jack Of All Trades - A role where there are multiple (usually 1-use actions) that the player can use. Can be either mafia-aligned or town-aligned.

K
KP - Kill Power - A role of either town or mafia alignment that has the power to kill another player at night.

L
LHF - Low Hanging Fruit - A player relatively easy to get hang as either alignment.

Lolcatting - When a mafia-aligned player is confirmed mafia, often mechanically through night actions, and starts posting random stuff.

LYLO - Hang or Lose - A game state where the town must hang a non-town-aligned player or they lose. This is because the town has majority by only one player.

M
Meta - Meta overall refers to the shared history and knowledge of each other's playing styles that comes from having played with each other across several games. A meta read is when someone relies on their knowledge of another player's past behavior in a certain alignment in order to form an informed read.

Modkill - When a player is killed by the host or moderator for rule breaking during a game. This can include breaking the rules on OGI, angleshooting, or behavioral issues.

Multi - A secondary account of someone else. Any multi's found on AoPS are permabanned.

Multi-ISO - Isolating the posts of multiple people so that only the posts of the selected people are outputted.

MYLO - Mishang and Lose - A gamestate where the town must hang a mafia-aligned player or vote for no hang in order not to lose. Town has majority by two players in this gamestate.

N
N1/N2… etc. - Night phase

NAI - Not Alignment Indicative - an action or post by a player which is not indicative of whether they are aligned with the town or mafia.

NK - Night Kill - The player(s) who were killed that night, either by mafia-aligned players or town-aligned players with killing power.

NL - No Hang - When no player is hang at the end of the day phase. A common strategy used during MYLO to force the mafia to kill another town member, narrowing down the pool of suspects.

O
Openwolving - When a player talks in a way that obviously indicates mafia behaviour.

OMGUS - Oh My Gosh You Suck - When a player votes someone because the other person voted them.

P
Peek - When a town investigative uses their night action on another player, and gets information on their alignment at the end of the night phase.

Pocketing - when a player tries to gain the townread of another player, usually made by mafia attempting to pocket town

POE - Process of Elimination - A strategy which attempts to identify players who aren’t cleared, and hang amongst those uncleared players in order to find mafia-aligned players. A list of who one wishes to hang are also often referred to as PoE.

PR - Power Role - A role that is not vanilla, and has special abilities in the setup. Can refer to both mafia-aligned and town-aligned players.

Q
N/A

R
Roleclaim - To claim your role in the thread, and is used as a tactic to avoid a hang or reveal that you are a PR.

RVS - Random Voting Stage - Occurs at the start of the first day phase, and is used to get the game started by randomly voting until solid evidence builds up. Often lasts until the middle of the first day phase, sometimes ends sooner.

RQS - Random Questioning Stage - Occurs at the start of the first day phase, and is used to get general information about players, like gender or past games. Often ends after all players have answered the questions, sometimes ends sooner.

RWSO - Rand Wolf, Sub Out - A belief that a player who rands mafia, or a specific player in general, is more likely to sub out after being randed in a mafia-aligned slot, when compared to being randed as a town aligned slot. Note that subbing out in a way that is impacted by alignment is against site rules, and so is speculation on why someone subbed out; people generally use this in a non-serious manner, and you shouldn’t be using it in a game.

S
Scum - Another word for mafia-aligned players

Scumbaiting - When a town-aligned player purposely acts like a PR in order to attract the night kill.

Scumhunting - When a player tries to deduce which players are mafia-aligned.

SOD - Start of Day

Soft Claim - to imply that you are a role without directly stating it in the thread. Also can be considered breadcrumbing - see Hard Claim, semi-relevant.

Spewing - Used to refer to when a flipped mafia-aligned player has given away information about who is or isn’t a member of the mafia (or when they're doing it while still alive).

T
Thunderdome - Two players attempting to get each other hanged with at least one attempting to force the game to hang between the two until one of them flips mafia, often a term used in LYLO where two players have already crossvoted; can also be shortened to "tdome".

Tinfoil - A theory or thought that relies on something highly unlikely being the case, often entertained or brought up out of paranoia.

TMI - Too Much Information - This is used as reasoning for someone being a wolf. It revolves around the idea that the post(s) the person is making are too well-informed, so they must be mafia since the mafia already know who the rest of the mafia are.

Tunnel - When a player is focusing on one other player, accusing only them of being scum and generally not trying to solve the rest of the game.

TWTBAW - Too Wolfy To Be A Wolf - This concept is where one person appears to be so suspicious that they seem too suspicious to be mafia-aligned, hence being Too Wolfy To Be A Wolf. This is often attributed to the fact that they would be more self-conscious about being so wolfy, and would be attempting to appear more like a town member if they were actually mafia-aligned.

U
N/A

V
VCA - Vote Count Analysis - Vote count analysis is a common strategy using previous votecounts to try to find town or scum, typically looking at player composition of wagons on certain players and what those players flipped, or if they have changed their vote to save certain players.

VT - Vanilla Townie - A town role with no special abilities.

Vanilla - A role with no special abilities (both Mafia Goon and Vanilla Townie fall under this category)

Villager - Another word for town-aligned player.

W
Wagon - Refers to the number of votes a player has that makes them a candidate for the hang.

WIFOM - Wine In Front Of Me - Term originating from this scene in The Princess Bride movie. Used to denote when someone is trying to guess or reason what choice a person made, when that person knew in advance that his/her choices would be subject to scrutiny.

WIL - Wants It Less - Refers to when players are showing a lack of desire to win. Often jokingly or mockingly used to denote players who played poorly.

WIM - Wants It More - Refers to the attribute of extreme passion/desire to win that some players possess.

WOAT - Worst of All Time

Wolf - Another word for mafia-aligned players.

X
N/A

Y
N/A

Z
N/A
